name: Butterscotch Almond Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 7 tbsp Unsalted butter, chilled and cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
Crust:
Preheat oven to 350°F. Line the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan with foil.
Butter and flour the foil.
Blend the first four ingredients in a food processor until nuts are finely chopped.
Add butter, egg yolk, and almond extract; blend until the mixture begins to gather together.
Press onto the bottom (not sides) of the prepared pan.
Bake until golden, about 25 minutes. Transfer to a rack and cool for 10 minutes.
Release the cake pan from the crust. Gently turn out the crust onto the rack,
peel off the foil, and cool. Place the crust back into the pan and reattach the pan sides.
Maintain oven temperature.
Filling:
Using an electric mixer, beat cream cheese with 1 1/4 cups sugar until well blended.
Stir in Scotch whisky and vanilla extract.
Add eggs one at a time, beating just until combined; pour into crust-lined pan.
Bake cheesecake until set at the edges but the center remains slightly jiggly when shaken, about 50 minutes.
Transfer to a rack and cool for 10 minutes.
Mix remaining 1/4 cup sugar with sour cream. Spoon this mixture over the cheesecake.
Bake for 10 minutes; transfer to a rack and cool for another 10 minutes.
Run a sharp knife around the top edge of the pan to loosen the cake, then release the pan sides.
Spoon the butterscotch topping over the cheesecake (save some for garnish) and sprinkle with chopped almonds.
Refrigerate up to 1 hour before serving.
